reviews.review_description: Love this.  It teaches how you do things you would actually do.  Can’t wait for the updates so I can continue learning more.
This app doesn't want to uninstall.

I wanted to try this app as another approach to my daily duolingo lesions. But as soon as I clicked to start IM HUNDREDSOF FEET IN THE AIR ON A TI Y FLOATING ISLAND!
Yup, not for me.

Can a developer give me some insight as to WHY.? It’s not even just this app. Sooooo dang many apps, THAT HAVE NOTHINGTO DO WITH FLYING, CLIFFS, ETC. all do the same thing. I’d expect to be up high if I was on a flight simulator, a free climbing app, or a space station, ( of course I’m NEVER going to, cause..ACROPHOBIA), but not to log onto a language class!
I’ve hoped outta soooo many things her in the VR world that for some dang reason put me standing on a cliff just to play the game. 

Nope, this is not for me. The anxiety is not worth the lesson.

Really fun lovely game, I dont leave reviews on things here but I have to say this is both fun and makes me learn, of course yeah its basic and wont get you astronomically far in your language journey but if you need somewhere to start or somewhere to train and expand your vocabulary whilst also having fun with games this app actually has quite a lot of content and levels for this.
I have tried the other language apps available on meta as well, and this is the only one with no paywall, completely free, I would 100% recommend this
My son and I both use this to practice our Dutch. It's helps build confidence in my knowledge

It’s just ok not that great from a person that’s been learning Japanese for a little bit I just wish there was a section were you can learn the writing systems for Chinese,Japanese and Korean instead of learning just how to say it and where you can talk to AI
Although I may have an older version, I still enjoy learning by doing. As someone else expressed earlier, it would be helpful to have the written material available to read.

The app itself is great, using it to learn Japanese. Too bad it isn't being developed anymore, the sitting/standing doesn't work correctly unfortunately. I play seated and constantly have to switch between sitting/standing to be able to grab certain object. A crouch button would do wonders. Also have a couple of times switchting between sitting/standing things go wrong. The height gets too low after a while - even when chosing standing I was almost on the ground.

reviews.review_title: Great concept, needs a lot of work still
reviews.review_description:
First of all, big red flag (no pun intended) is that you’re using the American flag for English? No. English doesn’t come from America, that’s just disrespectful.

Second of all, you don’t even tell the player how to move around. I had to guess and of course it’s that awful teleportation kind of movement rather than using the joystick. Absolutely hate it. Joystick movement would be so much better. Please provide instructions and also, A button instead of trigger for choosing things would be much better.

Thirdly, it overly repeats the same 3 tasks. Just asks me to do the same thing again and again. However I’ll assume that because the app is still in development that it may just need more adding.

I’ll check back in a few months and see if any of these issues are resolved.

developer_response.body:
Hi ahirusaru44, 

We appreciate your feedback. We are aware that the English language did not originate in the U.S. The vocabulary and accent more closely resemble the English spoken in the U.S., so in order to make that clear to users, we use the American flag. This way, they know the "flavor" of English that they will be exposed to in the app. We certainly didn't mean any disrespect to our "brothers and sisters across the pond". 

Smooth joystick is a movement option that can be toggled in the settings. I apologize that the settings options are not clear enough. I hope you are able to find and change them. 

The app can seem repetitive at first with the "Put things on/in other things.", depending on the user's level. Hopefully the later lessons provide you with a bit more variety. 

There will likely not be improvements in the future because we have run out of resources for further development, but we have removed the paywall, so hopefully it will still be able to add value to some users out there looking for a different experience. We appreciate your feedback and wish you the best on your language learning journey!

reviews.review_title: Language choices and in at the deep end
reviews.review_description:
The description on the quest site shows that it does Portuguese (Portugal) which most folk would take to mean European Portuguese. Unfortunately in the menu it only shows Portuguese (Brazil) and no way to switch from Brazillian to European.

The app could do with some preamble to explain the lessons and how they will work. I'm not sure what I was expecting, but constantly hearing the repeated  phrase "Put the spoon in the cup" in a language I don't yet know wasn't where I expected it to start

developer_response.body:
Hi DaftCat, 

Thank you for your feedback. We have found most of our Portuguese students to be studying Portuguese for the purpose of living/working/traveling in Brazil. Therefore, we have tried to match our Portuguese accent and vocabulary to the Portuguese used in Brazil. We apologize that we do not have a Portugal Portuguese option. Hopefully our Portuguese option can still add some value to users with the goal of communicating in Portugal, as Brazilians and Portuguese people can still communicate effectively with one another. 

Admittedly, we do not have a preamble to explain how the lessons work. There is a bit of a learning curve, but we designed it in this way purposefully to imitate the experience humans have learning our first language as children. We have found that some people absolutely love it and some people find it is not for them, but we felt this particular style and method were missing from the world, so hopefully it can provide some value to those who are looking specifically for this kind of experience. We appreciate your feedback.

reviews.review_title: it was great until the pay-wall
reviews.review_description: It was a great extracurricular tool for VR Language Learning as the games would give the students a bit of a brain break without removing the learning, but once the games got put behind the monthly pay-wall, it's just too much. If there was a one-time purchase for just the games, it'd be potentially doable, but not monthly.

developer_response.body: Hi Silphcolapras, thank you for your feedback. We have decided to make it all free for everyone. We hope the students enjoy it! Let us know if you have any issues accessing it.

reviews.review_description: It has a wonderful purpose, but with a lot of bugs and problems learning italian for exemple. Confratulations about your job, but, you should Take care about your user experience it is not good, many bugs, poor strategy, many mistakes, classes with no evolutivon...

developer_response.body: Hi Ezanatta, thank you for your feedback. We did the best we could with the resources we had. We hope it provided some value for you!

reviews.review_description: It's a very nice app for children to learn English. But I have a suggestion that you should change the way you charge if you want more people to use your app. Monthly fees may deter many potential customers.

developer_response.body: Hi berty.li.988, thank you for your feedback. We have made it free for everyone now. Let us know if you have any issues accessing it. Enjoy!

reviews.review_description:
I agree that immersive learning is the way to go, but opened it up and randomly chose Hebrew just to check it out and was immediately greeted with a woman putting a spoon inside of a cup and saying it in the language I chose. Or at least I thought so. I mimic'd what she was doing but nothing happened. She just kept repeating the same sentence over and over again. I finally exited and chose English to see what the deal was. 

The English version said "Put the spoon in the cup" which I did and it worked fine so I was pretty lost. I tried again in a new language and despite following the visual direction of putting the spoon in the cup, nothing happened.

There are all sorts of rooms and tables with various items on them that you can pick up and learn what they are but I could not find a path to take so I assume that for now, you just walk around and click on random things to hear how to say it? I wish when you opened the app there was an explanation about how it works. Looking foward to seeing this develop. It is a great idea.

developer_response.body:
Hi cvan65, thank you for your feedback. We apologize that your experience wasn't ideal. The woman is putting the spoon in the cup saying, "I put the spoon in the cup." She then points at you and says, "Put the fork in the cup". The idea is that users hear the word "fork" when picking up the fork and hear the word "cup" when picking up the cup and are able to infer the meaning. 

Based on your feedback, we will add some instructions or a short demonstration to show how it works and the lesson order. There is a gold trail on the ground leading the user, but it is not obvious enough at the moment, so we will fix that also. 

Right now, we are heavily focused on improving the instructions, lesson order, and progress meter to make everything clearer and easier to understand and to follow. We are happy to hear that you like the idea, and we hope you will give it another try in a month. We update it regularly. Thanks!

reviews.review_description:
First impressions are great. 
But it would be much more effectiv if you put the article with the defining gender in front of the words.
In German or French it is nessary to learn them from the start.( Der die das /le la les)
An other helpfull feature would be to see the written word while hearing it.
Thx for your great work!
Many thanks to the developers. Keep up the good work

The idea of ​​the program is great, I like it,
Thank u

reviews.review_title: Good with potential to be amazing
reviews.review_description: Only suggestion/criticism i have to offer would be something written to follow along with the audio. I have learned the words that are covered in the topic but the whole sentence spoken I am not sure of how its spelled out, or how to properly pronounce some. Something like karaoke has for the learning sentences would make the learning experience that much more effective in my opinion. Very immersive and practical so far, thank you

developer_response.body: You can press pause with the "hamburger" (three lines) button on the left Quest controller and toggle the picture that shows the magnifying glass on the speech bubble. That will turn on the subtitles as you work through the lessons. For languages like Japanese/Chinese, we only have characters instead of Pinyin/Romanji, but for Spanish, Portuguese, Italian, etc., it should be just what you're looking for. We will create a tutorial later that makes those settings easier to find. Hope that helps, and we're glad you like it! We have a lot of new things on the way, so stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: Enjoy this app so far just wish it had me do more speaking but it’s really good for learning to like listen to another language and understand it

reviews.review_title: Most intuitive way to learn a language
reviews.review_description:
Great language learning app that relies on listening, repetition, and life-like interaction in VR. Interacting with and seeing objects move while completing small tasks as prompted in the target language in a zero-stress, zero- embarrassment environment makes learning much quicker and words and phrases easier to remember. For foundational communication skills this is the ideal way to learn. It’s much more natural than learning through a phone app, where interaction is limited to tapping on text and icons. 

For beginners to beginner-intermediate level this app is great for listening and speaking. Hope to see this in the main Meta app store soon, and would be more than happy to pay for a subscription.
